channel.title: DutchNews.nl
channel.description: DutchNews.nl brings daily news from The Netherlands in English

item.title: Thuisbezorgd hides costs and fakes discounts: consumer group
item.description:
Thuisbezorgd hides costs and fakes discounts: consumer group

July 22, 2026

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

Add as a favourite source on Google Add DutchNews as a favourite source on Google

The food delivery platform Thuisbezorgd.nl hides compulsory charges and advertises fake discounts, the Dutch consumer association Consumentenbond said on Tuesday, calling on the company to stop and to compensate affected customers.

Mandatory costs should be clearly shown before a customer starts ordering, the association said, but Thuisbezorgd’s app and website do not reveal compulsory “service” charges – for items such as bags, plastic packaging and sauce – until part-way through or at the end of an order, adding an average of €2 to €4 to the bill.

The association also said the company broke rules on special offers. Under Authority for Consumers and Markets (ACM) rules, a struck-through sale price must be the lowest the seller charged in the previous 30 days. In one case, the association found Indian snacks that had previously cost €3.75 being offered “on sale” from €7.50 to €6.

“We have been in talks with Thuisbezorgd since March, but the platform is still confronting customers with hidden costs and fake discounts,” said Consumentenbond director Sandra Molenaar, noting the company has more than six million users in the Netherlands.

Because the practice has probably been going on for years, the association estimates the hidden costs run into the hundreds of millions of euros in total. A Thuisbezorgd spokesperson said the company did not recognise the association’s accusations.

The Consumentenbond said it had also found hidden costs at Uber Eats, which made changes in June, though no fake discounts. The findings are part of a wider campaign against hidden charges and misleading offers that also named Bol and Amazon last week.

The association can press companies to change but cannot impose penalties; that is a matter for the ACM, which has fined firms including Leen Bakker, G-Star and Jysk in the past.
item.dc:creator: Louis Gore-Langton

item.title: Three arrested over €5m smartphone heist near Schiphol
item.description:
Three arrested over €5m smartphone heist near Schiphol

July 22, 2026

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

Add as a favourite source on Google Add DutchNews as a favourite source on Google

Three men have been arrested over the theft of a shipment of smartphones with an estimated street value of around €5m from the Schiphol area, the Royal Military Police (Koninklijke Marechaussee) have said.

The suspects are two 24-year-old men from Amsterdam and a 39-year-old from Zoetermeer.

The shipment went missing on June 18, prompting an investigation, led by the Noord-Holland public prosecution department, into an organised crime group.

One of the suspects works for a transport company at Schiphol and is thought to have passed confidential information to the group, the military police said. Further arrests have not been ruled out.
item.dc:creator: Louis Gore-Langton

item.title: Two trams collide on Rotterdam’s Erasmus Bridge, 15 injured
item.description:
Two trams collide on Rotterdam’s Erasmus Bridge, 15 injured

July 22, 2026

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

Add as a favourite source on Google Add DutchNews as a favourite source on Google

Fifteen people were injured, one of them seriously, when two trams collided on the Erasmus Bridge in Rotterdam on Wednesday morning, the regional safety board has said. One of the trams was derailed by the impact.

The crash happened at around 11.10am, regional broadcaster Rijnmond reported. Footage shows one tram coming to a halt as it drove onto the bridge from the direction of the city centre, before a second tram approached from behind and ran into it, apparently without braking.

The seriously injured person was trapped and had to be freed before being taken to hospital. The safety board initially reported 18 injured and two seriously hurt, but later revised the figures.

Transport company RET said it could not yet say what caused the rear-end collision, adding that it was shocked by the footage and that its thoughts were with the victims and passengers. Two cars were also damaged, the fire service said.

The power to the overhead lines was switched off and the bridge closed to traffic, halting tram services on lines 3 and 5.
item.dc:creator: Louis Gore-Langton

item.title: Man jailed for 15 months over threats to Groningen butchers
item.description:
Man jailed for 15 months over threats to Groningen butchers

July 22, 2026

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

Add as a favourite source on Google Add DutchNews as a favourite source on Google

A Groningen court has sentenced a 23-year-old man to 15 months in prison for sending death threats to butchers in the province and neighbouring Drenthe.

Prosecutors requested a four-year sentence, saying he had a terrorist motive. But the court ruled there was insufficient evidence for such a charge, saying instead he acted out of radical environmental and animal-rights beliefs.

Because four months of his sentence are suspended, the man, known as Bas V., will be released immediately, having already served his time in pre-trial detention. He is bound by a three-year probationary period.

The crime

Last September, Bas V. sent threatening emails to 12 butcher shops demanding they close. “You get one warning, nothing more. If the shop isn’t closed tomorrow, I will come with a machete and stab everyone to death,” read the email.

The court stated: “The texts on his phone say something about his mindset, but not that he wanted to disrupt society with them.”

Bas V., who admitted to sending the emails, was arrested after seven of the butchers filed a complaint. Police found a machete in his home and notes on his phone saying he also needed a pistol with a silencer to “kill people responsible for the humiliation of animals,” according to broadcaster NOS.

The national broadcaster also says that Bas V. was found by experts to have a gaming addiction, a cannabis addiction, ADHD and autism, and will be placed in a protected living facility to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Comiteau

item.title: House prices rising fastest in Groningen, slowest in Amsterdam
item.description:
House prices rising fastest in Groningen, slowest in Amsterdam

July 22, 2026

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

Add as a favourite source on Google Add DutchNews as a favourite source on Google

House prices are climbing fastest in Groningen and slowest in the Randstad, figures from the national statistics office CBS and the Land Registry showed on Tuesday.

Homes in Groningen province cost 7.9% more in the second quarter than a year earlier – the biggest rise in the country – while in Noord-Holland prices went up just 2.4%.

House prices rose 4.2% nationwide, the tenth quarter running that they have climbed, though more slowly than the 5.2% jump in the first three months of the year. After Groningen, the steepest rises were in Drenthe, Gelderland and Friesland, all above 5%.

Of the four biggest cities, The Hague rose most, at 4.5%, while Amsterdam barely moved, up 0.8%. Fewer homes sold than a year earlier in both Amsterdam and Rotterdam.

Nearly 59,000 homes changed hands across the country, 2.7% more than the same quarter last year.
item.dc:creator: Louis Gore-Langton

item.title: Dutch government warns against travel to the Middle East
item.description:
Dutch government warns against travel to the Middle East

July 22, 2026

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

Add as a favourite source on Google Add DutchNews as a favourite source on Google

As the conflict between the US and Iran escalates, the foreign affairs ministry has tightened its advice for people wanting to travel to the region.

With Iran targeting US allies in the Middle East in response to American attacks, the Dutch government has advised against travelling to Qatar, Jordan, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait and the United Arab Emirates – which includes the popular holiday destination of Dubai – unless absolutely necessary.

That falls under the ministry’s orange travel advisory, which discourages all travel unless there’s an emergency, such as the death of a family member.

The travel advice for Bahrain has moved up to red, meaning the ministry says the Dutch embassy cannot offer help. “Whatever your situation is, don’t travel there,” the ministry said. “It’s too dangerous.”

The US, UK, Canada and Australia have also updated their travel alerts for Gulf countries in recent days.

Check the ministry’s website for the latest travel advisories.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Comiteau

item.title: Explosive attacks fall as task force targets young offenders
item.description:
Explosive attacks fall as task force targets young offenders

July 22, 2026

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

Add as a favourite source on Google Add DutchNews as a favourite source on Google

Attacks using explosives fell by more than 17% in the first half of this year, the first substantial drop since the wave of explosions peaked in 2024, according to the government’s Offensive against Explosions (OTE) working group.

The number of explosions or attempted explosions decreased from 705 during the first half of 2025 to 581 during the same time, putting the OTE on track to achieve a 10% decrease this year.

But explosions remain a serious safety threat – and it’s mostly young people who use them to commit crimes. Almost half of all arrested suspects were minors, reports NU.nl, with fewer than one in six suspects older than 30. A large proportion of offenders are under the age of 25.

“Every attack is one too many,” said Carola Schouten, mayor of Rotterdam and chairwoman of the OTE. “But it is good to see that the number of attacks is starting to decrease nationwide… This requires continued attention and joint commitment on all fronts.”

Young offenders

Specifically, the task force is focusing on reducing the number of young offenders, finding and prosecuting those who hire them and their intermediaries and limiting the availability of explosives – in this case mostly powerful, illegally trafficked fireworks, often procured from neighbouring countries like Germany or ordered online.

“This is done, among other things, by disrupting the production, transport, storage and trade in such explosives,” said the OTE, which is also setting up a joint Europol task force with Germany and Sweden, and lobbying with France and Sweden for tighter EU rules on powerful fireworks.

But given the young age of offenders, prevention is the name of the game. The OTE has begun a pilot focusing on conflict mediation among young people, while parents, caregivers and those who work with them are being given support to make “young people more resilient to (online) temptations to crime”.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Comiteau

item.title: Van Weel in Ghana to rally West African pressure on Bolle Jos
item.description:
Van Weel in Ghana to rally West African pressure on Bolle Jos

July 22, 2026

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

Add as a favourite source on Google Add DutchNews as a favourite source on Google

The Netherlands is trying to build a coalition of West African countries to pressure Sierra Leone into handing over fugitive drugs baron Jos “Bolle Jos” Leijdekkers, justice minister David van Weel said after a visit to Ghana, in an interview with the Telegraaf.

Van Weel told the paper that Sierra Leone’s neighbours, including Liberia and Ghana, are increasingly frustrated at the shelter the country is giving Leijdekkers, whose network operates across the region.

“We are not ruling out any option,” he said, hinting at measures he could not discuss publicly. The Netherlands also wants to set up a permanent secretariat in West Africa to tackle drug smuggling networks, he said.

Sierra Leone’s justice minister, Alpha Sesay, attended the talks on his own initiative and has written to Van Weel proposing to work towards a solution – a sign, according to the Dutch minister, that the pressure is being felt, though he said he saw more willingness to talk than to act.

Leijdekkers, 34, is the Netherlands’ most wanted criminal, sentenced in absentia to prison terms in the Netherlands and Belgium totalling around 80 years for large-scale cocaine smuggling and ordering a murder. He has also been ordered to repay €96 million to the Dutch state.

He is reported to be living under presidential protection in Sierra Leone, where he is said to have a child with the daughter of president Julius Maada Bio. His network has been linked to a record cocaine seizure off the Canary Islands in May, one of the largest single hauls in history.

The regional approach follows a series of failed attempts to get him. Dutch special forces twice came close to arresting him at sea before the operations were called off, and a Dutch push to cut EU development aid to Sierra Leone has so far failed to win support from other member states, ministers told MPs earlier this month.

Prosecutors have meanwhile targeted his family, with his brother extradited from Turkey in June on money laundering charges.
item.dc:creator: Louis Gore-Langton

item.title: Man arrested after fatal shooting near Vierdaagse festivities
item.description:
Man arrested after fatal shooting near Vierdaagse festivities

July 22, 2026

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

Add as a favourite source on Google Add DutchNews as a favourite source on Google

Police in Nijmegen have arrested a man in connection with a fatal shooting on Tuesday evening, just outside the area where the Vierdaagsefeesten, the street festivities accompanying the annual four-day marches, were taking place.

A young man was shot at around 10pm on the Graafseweg and died later in hospital despite the efforts of ambulance crews and a trauma helicopter team, broadcaster NOS reported. The arrested man is suspected of involvement in the shooting, police said. The circumstances remain unclear.

The city centre was busy at the time because of the marches, which began on Tuesday, and the accompanying festivities. Police told news agency ANP they saw no link between the shooting and the event itself.

The area around the Graafseweg remained cordoned off on Wednesday morning for forensic investigation. Police are looking into whether more people were involved and have appealed for witnesses to come forward.
item.dc:creator: Louis Gore-Langton

item.title: Police seize servers of porn site Motherless in abuse inquiry
item.description:
Police seize servers of porn site Motherless in abuse inquiry

July 21, 2026

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

See more DutchNews articles in your Google search results

Add as a favourite source on Google Add DutchNews as a favourite source on Google

Police have raided the hosting company of the pornography website Motherless at locations in Steenbergen, Rotterdam and Amsterdam, seizing servers used by the site, broadcaster NOS has reported.

The public prosecution department is investigating images of possible sexual abuse that appeared on the site, including images of child sexual abuse and of women being assaulted while drugged. Both the people who uploaded the material and the role of the site itself are under investigation, which will be carried out with Europol.

No one has been arrested so far.

Motherless, which had tens of millions of visitors a month, was taken offline by prosecutors in May after reporting by NOS and Nieuwsuur revealed large amounts of illegal material on its servers, hosted by Steenbergen-based provider Nforce. The site later reappeared online via other routes.

The raids are the latest step in a series of Dutch investigations connected to the site. In June, NOS revealed that moderators of the site had themselves posted images of child sexual abuse, with one employee alone uploading 110,000 images.

Earlier, an investigation by CNN had found some 20,000 videos on the site of women being filmed while drugged or asleep, and NRC reported that Dutch users were among those sharing such material.
item.dc:creator: Louis Gore-Langton
